Mod - 7Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 3
Speaker 1: Hey guys, and welcome to the workbook for module seven, this is the closing sequence,
and as you've just seen in the main lesson, an effective closing sequence can literally be
the make or break of your launch. You know, up to 80% of your sales can come in these
final hours, so it pays to pay some attention here, and really integrate what you're
learning, and use the theme plates that we're going to be talking about in just a bit.
While surface level time based urgency from a countdown can be effective to bring in
sales, the real opportunity to amplify our sales by an even bigger, greater degree is in
countering a more insidious conversion killer, which is indecisiveness as well as the
perception that a choice doesn't even need to be made, that a launch can just end in a
whimper, without them making a decision with just kind of that crappy little countdown
timer hitting zero, and then kind of wiping your hands clean like it never happened.
So, that is what we are trying to avoid here. We are instead trying to coach decisiveness,
and there are many tools we have at our disposal to do it. So, what gets in the way of
decision making, sometimes are cognitive biases, so we can acknowledge and remove
them by self forgiving past actions or inactions.
If you have the six figure email pack, you will see market search for meaning, which is a
great way to do this. Coaching decisiveness through exclusive empowerment that this
time to use and benefit from your product, just how we use that as the call out to get
people in your funnel to begin with, we can use it on the back end, to really make
people feel exclusively empowered, and privileged to use your product based on all
those other sources we've discussed earlier in the course.
We're also going to continue to facilitate the identification with being a buyer, we're
going to introduce the concept of the minimum viable commitment, we spoke about
this in the module before. But setting the bar just high enough to make it clear, but also
not making it to that, they need to be 1,010% confident and you know, get married
today, and live happily ever after, you know. What's a minimum buyable commitment
for being in your program? We are going to support that with a quick, valuable win,
which is something tangible that they can receive, that ROI that they can receive within
hours or days.
If what you're doing doesn't have a monetary aspect to it, what is the million dollar pain
that it solves? What's that quick, crunchy valuable win they're going to be able to enjoy
in the minutes and hours after joining a program? And of course, where coaching binary
choice, the fact that a choice does exist, and we're going to say this pretty much literally
in this sequence. There's no room to beat around the bush. We want them to know that
a decision has to be made, they owe it to themselves to make a conscious choice.
But we're going to enhance their ability to do it by giving them a sense of control,
collaboration, and even co creation in that final close, and you'll see how that plays out
in a few minutes, we're also going to be coaching while we're coaching decisiveness
through the use of second hand FOMO. Show our own fear of them missing out. Make
the buying decision about more than just them. You know, their decision doesn't exist in

the silo, it effects us in our mission, their family, their friends, their you know,
stakeholders in their life.
So that second hand FOMO is a way to coach decisiveness, and we will wrap this up with
risk reversal, which combined with the risk revealer that we spoke about in the last
module, creates this dynamic of risk mitigation, which step, which action or inaction is
going to be the less risky. So, that is what we're doing to coach decisiveness, it's time to
explore some questions that are going to really help fuel the content for the theme
plates we're about to write.
So, you've answered some of these before, and we've got some additional ones. These
might sound familiar, so going back, what about your market struggles breaks your
heart? That you're going to use when you really channel that second hand FOMO, and
what reality do you see for them that they don't yet see for themselves? And what pains
you most about their inability to see it?
So those are really going to be you invoking your own FOMO, towards the end, and
making that decision more than just about them. Some additional questions, complete
the following sentence, you're in an exclusively empowered position to use my product
and achieve promised outcome if ... So we've already done a lot of wok on exclusive
empowerment, I'm not going to dive into the mechanics of it now. There's lessons on it,
back in what was it, module three I believe, so go back to that if you need a primer on
exclusive empowerment right now. What we're doing right now is using it within the
frame of using your product, and achieving that outcome.
Then what's that minimum viable commitment, is it test it risk free for 30 days? Put the
lessons in module one into practice? Go through the first audio, use it once, invest $1
today. So you just really kind of want to lower that bar, it's not about you're joining
forever, and you've got to put 15 hours a week and be 120% committed, like you really
want to allow this to be inclusive of their doubts and skepticism about either you,
themselves, or their product, or all these beliefs you've reshifted.
You know, you've done a lot of work in creating those new beliefs, but maybe the paint
has been fully dried out yet, maybe there's still some leakage, maybe there's still some
doubt spilling to the floor, and you want to allow that to be okay. Allow that paint to dry
after they've entered your program. So, tell them it's all good if they're still a little bit
concerned. You know.
Just simply go through the first audio, see if it's for you. So that is the minimum viable
commitment, I want you to decide what that looks like for your product. Next is the
quickest and most valuable win tat they'll achieve within hours or days of getting
started. We've covered this, I'm not going to into more right now, but that's something
you're going to want to be armed with by the time you start writing these theme plates.
Then we're going to move into what's that ownership experience within minutes of your
prospect enrolling. What will they see, what will they be doing, how can you pre pave
that reality to make it more familiar and more safe? What positive qualities or

Page 3 of 3
characteristics have they exhibited in the past that got them to this point? You know,
can you reflect back those qualities to remind them of the power they have to decide
and overcome resistance?
You're going to see how this plays out in the Danny Tanner theme plate. So it's just
giving them a chance to see that they have this ability in them, they've already
demonstrated in the past, and now it's not a matter of being someone they're not, but
just channeling this better part of themselves, that they're fully capable of. And do you
have any stories of someone who got in last minute, and then became a breakthrough
success story?
So this is really good for continuing that identification with being a buyer aspect, you
know, especially if they could see that you know, not all success stories are those super
charged action takers that got in on day one. You know, is there anyone who turned
their life around by just sneaking in under the gate last second Indiana Jones style?
Because that would be more inspiring and more likely for them to identify with, at this
stage of your launch.
So that is what we're going to be working with, those are the questions that I want you
to take a few minutes right now to explore and write your answers, because it's going to
serve us a lot when it comes to writing the theme plates coming up. So, take a few
minutes to do that, and I'll meet you soon in the closing theme plates.
